results.title: Rex: AI Operations Partner for Enterprise Order to Cash | Y Combinator
results.content:
Rex is an AI-native service for order to cash. We replace people-heavy operations with AI agents that continuously manage invoices, customers and exceptions. Order to cash is a $60B outsourced function because the work is too messy for traditional automation. Every customer has different context, exceptions do not follow clean rules, and the next step is often buried in emails, portals, handoffs, or someone’s memory. Rex agents run continuously in the background and resolve work to SLA standards so cash keeps moving. We're live with pre-IPO tech companies, managing >$500m in receivables from F100 companies, cutting manual follow-up cycles from weeks to hours. We founded Rex after spending four years building Sequence, an a16z-backed finance ops platform, from zero to Series A — where we [...] Merlin Kafka

Merlin Kafka

Twitter account

Founder

Merlin is the Founder and CEO of Rex, the AI partner for enterprise order-to-cash. He spent 4 years as CPO at Sequence (a16z-backed), an order-to-cash platform where he grew the company to millions in ARR and hundreds of customers. He has deep domain expertise in order to cash and accounts receivable workflows -- the exact space Rex is transforming with AI. Previously a PM at Tessian (Sequoia-backed, acquired in 2023). Studied Industrial Engineering in Cambridge and Product Design in Glasgow.

Hey Product Hunt, I'm Merlin, co-founder of Rex. Lewis and I spent four years building AR software at Sequence. What we kept seeing: someone on the finance team copying data from a portal into a spreadsheet into NetSuite, every single day. Automation tools never fixed it because the work is full of exceptions and judgment calls. So we built Rex to just do the work. Agents plug into your existing stack and run collections, portal uploads, and the AR inbox end to end. Anything sensitive gets routed to your team. Synthesia runs their AR on us today. Ask me anything, including what the agents still get wrong.

results.title: Gaviti Alternatives: 2026 AR Automation Comparison | Rex
results.content:
## Questions to ask in a demo

Every vendor in these categories will say their tool is automated and uses AI. The way to separate a scheduled playbook from an agent is to make them show you on a live account.

## Where Rex fits

Rex is an agentic AI accounts receivable agent. It replaces manual playbook execution with an autonomous agent, going further than workflow automation and analytics. Where an analytics tool tells your team which accounts to chase and a playbook fires the next scheduled step, Rex reads the state of each account and decides what to do, then does it. [...] Agentic AI agents. Where Rex sits. Instead of automating a playbook, an agent reads each account and decides the next action, then takes it. It reads replies, handles disputes, applies cash, and escalates only what needs a human. It is accountable for the result, not the activity.

### Matching a category to your situation [...] # Gaviti Alternatives: 2026 AR Automation Comparison

A category-level guide to Gaviti alternatives for AR automation in 2026, comparing workflow and analytics tools, portals, and agentic AI agents.

Gaviti Alternatives: 2026 AR Automation Comparison

Teams comparing Gaviti alternatives are usually deciding how much of the collections work they still want to do themselves. Gaviti is publicly known as an AR collections workflow-automation and analytics tool. Its alternatives split into clear categories, from portals to agentic AI agents, and the choice comes down to whether you want playbooks and dashboards to run, or an agent that runs the work for you.
